name: Loguru
what_it_does: Drop-in Python logging library that replaces the standard logging module with a simpler API, automatic colorized output, log rotation, structured serialization, and exception tracebacks with variable values.
use_cases: Structured agent execution logging with automatic serialization to JSON for ingestion into log aggregation systems
Capturing full exception context including variable values in agent tracebacks for post-mortem debugging
Rotating log files by size or time for long-running agent processes without filesystem overflow
Filtering log output per sink so debug logs go to file while only warnings go to stdout in production
Intercepting standard library logging from third-party dependencies and routing through a unified Loguru pipeline
not_for: Distributed tracing across microservices — Loguru logs local context only and does not emit spans or traces for OpenTelemetry
High-throughput applications where logging overhead matters — Loguru's rich formatting is heavier than raw stdlib logging
Teams that require strict compliance with Python logging ecosystem standards like structlog or OpenTelemetry SDK handlers
best_when: You want zero-config, human-readable structured logging with rich exception context and log rotation for Python agents or scripts.
avoid_when: Your stack requires full OpenTelemetry compatibility or you are integrating with an existing stdlib logging configuration that third-party libraries depend on.

auth.notes: Local Python library, no auth required

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Loguru's exception formatting with local variable values is best-in-class for Python debugging; serialize=True produces clean JSON for log aggregators
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Logging operations are append-only and side-effect free relative to application logic
known_agent_gotchas: Global logger singleton means library code that imports loguru shares the same logger — use logger.bind() to add context without polluting library logs
Intercepting stdlib logging requires explicit logging.basicConfig + PropagateHandler setup or third-party library logs will bypass Loguru entirely
serialize=True outputs one JSON object per line but the format is not configurable — downstream parsers must handle Loguru's specific schema
logger.remove() removes ALL handlers including the default stderr sink — always call remove() with the handler ID, not bare remove()
Async logging sinks are not natively supported; heavy I/O sinks can block the event loop unless enqueue=True is set for background thread dispatch

security_score_components.security_notes: No network layer; secure by default. Caution: serialize=True will log all local variables including secrets if exception occurs inside a secrets-handling function.
